items.q: What defines epic poetry as a genre?
items.correct: Long narrative poems on heroic subjects, in elevated style, often invoking a muse, in medias res openings, and treating events of national or cosmic significance.
items.misconception: Epic means any poem that is very long.
items.random_wrong: Epic means any story with a battle in it.
items.why: Length alone isn't enough. Epic has specific conventions (invocation, elevated diction, heroic subject) inherited from Homer through Virgil to Milton — students reduce it to scale.

items.q: What is the structure of a Shakespearean sonnet?
items.correct: Fourteen lines in iambic pentameter, structured as three quatrains and a final couplet, rhyming ABAB CDCD EFEF GG, often with a volta near the couplet.
items.misconception: A sonnet is any short love poem.
items.random_wrong: A sonnet must always be exactly ten lines long.
items.why: Students treat sonnet as a vague genre. It is a strict 14-line form with specific rhyme schemes — and sonnets need not be about love (think Hopkins's religious sonnets).

items.correct: Attributing human emotions or qualities to nature or inanimate objects to reflect a character's feelings or scene's mood (e.g. angry storms during conflict).
items.misconception: Pathetic fallacy is when an argument in a story is weak or sad.
items.random_wrong: Pathetic fallacy is when characters cry over silly things.
items.why: The term uses 'pathetic' in its old sense — pertaining to emotion (pathos) — not 'feeble'. Students misread it as a value judgement when it is a technical device coined by Ruskin.

items.q: An epistolary novel is told through?
items.why: It's Letters. “Verse” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Garcia Marquez is associated with which subgenre?
items.why: It's Magical realism. “Bildungsroman” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was One Hundred Years of Solitude published?
items.why: The real figure is 1967. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
